reasoning: Transpacific eastbound flight advantages mirror the transatlantic case and are equally well-documented. The North Pacific subtropical jet stream (typically 100-250 kt, seasonally strongest in winter) creates significant time savings on Tokyo/Seoul→US routes. Airlines have used this operationally since Pan Am's first transpacific services. Unlike the Atlantic where flights can partially avoid the jet, the Pacific's width makes jet stream routing even more critical — the "great circle" route for westbound flights often dips significantly south to avoid headwinds. This is standard atmospheric dynamics (Coriolis effect + meridional temperature gradient). Registration 2026-03-28 postdates publicly available flight data by decades.
